Likely need to get definitions of schema version constants. Could target them via graph.find with logical_key_regex? Or use session.expand_file with depth to get source lines.

The instructions: Use exact short ids when referring to graph nodes. Short ids look like F1, S3, D2, R1. In graph relations we have many symbols with short ids? Not given but likely they have IDs like S1, S2 etc. The graph relations list shows [S1] etc. Those are probably short ids. We need to refer to them with exact short ids as provided. The frontier recommends expand_file [F1] which is the file node. We might need to expand with depth to get symbols. Already done earlier but maybe need to get more details. Could use session.expand_file with target selector "crate:graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs" but we need short id? The file node likely has short id F1. So we can call session.expand_file with payload targeting F1. That will expand symbols.

Thus tool_call: session.expand_file@v1 with payload {"target":"F1"} maybe also depth? The contract says payload includes target selector and optional depth, limit, etc. Use minimal.

We need to output JSON only. Kind: tool_call. tool_call.tool_name: "session.expand_file@v1". Payload: {"target":"F1"} maybe also "depth":2? But we can keep simple.

But we are about to call tool to get evidence. The readiness_state should reflect that we are not ready to edit yet. Maybe "evidence_visible"? Actually after expand we will have evidence. But we haven't acquired yet. So readiness_state could be "not_ready". The contract: When kind is tool_call, readiness_state can be "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". Since we haven't executed, we are not ready, so "not_ready". Or maybe "evidence_visible"? The evidence is not yet visible. I'd put "not_ready".
